channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Suspected US airstrikes in Yemen kill at least 6 people, Houthi rebels say
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Suspected U.S. airstrikes battered rebel-controlled areas of Yemen into Wednesday, with the Houthis saying the attacks killed at least six people across the country.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: 5 things to know about Republicans’ new blueprint to pass Trump's agenda
item.description: Senate Republicans on Wednesday rolled out a 70-page blueprint to advance President Trump’s tax agenda, along with directions that could pave the way for more than a trillion dollars in cuts to government spending.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Senate budget blueprint empowers GOP chair to decide if Trump tax cuts add to deficit
item.description: Senate Republicans on Wednesday unveiled a 70-page budget resolution that they say would give Senate Budget Committee Chair Lindsey Graham (R-S.C.), a staunch ally of President Trump, the power to determine whether extending the 2017 Trump tax cuts officially adds to the federal deficit.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Montana's anti-transgender bathroom restrictions are on hold under a judge's order
item.description: HELENA, Mont. (AP) — A judge on Wednesday temporarily blocked a Montana law that restricts transgender people's use of bathrooms in public buildings. The measure, which Republican Gov.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Police say the 2023 Nashville school shooter hid mental health issues from doctors and family
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — The shooter behind the 2023 Nashville elementary school attack that killed six people, including three children, had been obsessively planning it for years while hiding mental health issues from family and doctors, a police report released Wednesday reveals.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN MATTISE, KIMBERLEE KRUESI and TRAVIS LOLLER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Charges dismissed against Illinois man accused of assault by Rep. Nancy Mace
item.description: Federal prosecutors moved to dismiss charges Tuesday against a man U.S. Rep. Nancy Mace (R-S.C.) accused of assaulting her during an event at the Capitol last year.
item.dc:creator: Sophie Brams, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: SEE IT: NASA astronaut Sunita Williams reunites with dogs after 9-month stay in space
item.description: NASA astronaut Suni Williams returned to Earth after a planned eight-day mission turned into a nine-month stay on the ISS.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Delandro, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: HHS fires entire staff of program that helps low-income people afford heat and A/C
item.description: The firings come as HHS fired thousands of people as part of the Trump administration’s efforts to cut staffing for federal agencies.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, NewsNation

item.title: US immigration will only recognize 2 genders going forward, ends Biden-era practice
item.description: U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services said it's updating its policies to clarify that it only recognizes two biological sexes, male and female.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Beloved Anchorage pet reindeer euthanized after someone tampered with pen, possibly poisoned him
item.description: ANCHORAGE, Alaska (AP) — An iconic pet reindeer beloved by many in Alaska's largest city has been euthanized, just weeks after someone tampered with his cage and possibly poisoned him, his caretaker said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: MARK THIESSEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: NFL to use camera technology to measure first downs next season, approves more rule changes
item.description: The NFL announced a multitude of rule changes on Tuesday, including a new way to measure first downs for the 2025 season.
item.dc:creator: Jared Bush, Fox 5

item.title: April Fool's prank renames French Quarter to American Quarter in New Orleans
item.description: An April Fool’s prank left many New Orleans residents and visitors stunned after a fake website announced that the historic French Quarter had been renamed the “American Quarter” by executive order of Louisiana Gov. Jeff Landry.
item.dc:creator: Kelvin Spears, KRON4

item.title: Why Tesla may avoid the blow of Trump's auto tariffs
item.description: Elon Musk’s focus on American-made products at Tesla could shield his company from the brunt of President Donald Trump’s new tariffs on foreign vehicles and automobile parts.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Republicans win Florida special elections in Trump strongholds by narrower margins than in 2024
item.description: ORMOND BEACH, Fla. (AP) — Republicans Jimmy Patronis and Randy Fine won special elections Tuesday in two Florida congressional districts, bolstered by President Donald Trump’s endorsement to fill vacant seats in reliably Republican strongholds.
item.dc:creator: STEPHANY MATAT, KATE PAYNE and ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Bodycam in Connecticut house of horrors shows suspect after stepson's fiery escape
item.description: A Waterbury, Connecticut police officer asked alleged child abuser Kimberly Sullivan if her stepson "has free rein" to leave his room in bodycam footage after her house fire.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Man called 'Scamdaddy' sentenced for defrauding Utah-based credit unions out of $450K
item.description: A man who called himself "Scamdaddy" was sentenced for his involvement in a scheme to defraud Utah credit unions out of more than $450,000.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: Booker sets record for longest Senate speech in history
item.description: Sen. Cory Booker (D-N.J.) on Tuesday set a new record for longest floor speech in Senate history, having held the floor for 25 hours and 5 minutes to decry potential GOP spending cuts in their looming tax bill and policies put in place by the Trump administration. Booker, the No.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, KRON4

item.title: Trump administration admits it wrongly deported man to prison in El Salvador
item.description: The Trump administration acknowledged late Monday that it had mistakenly deported a Salvadoran man protected from removal, sending him to a facility in El Salvador where they argue they are unable to secure his return.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KTLA 5

item.title: Judge rejects Trump administration’s bid to move Mahmoud Khalil’s legal case to Louisiana
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A federal judge has ruled that the legal battle over Mahmoud Khalil’s deportation should continue to play out in New Jersey, rejecting the Trump administration’s bid to transfer the Columbia University protester’s case to Louisiana. In a written decision Tuesday, U.S.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Most immigrants at risk of deportation from US are Christian, report finds
item.description: As many as four in five immigrants at risk of deportation from the United States are Christian, according to a new report that calls on their fellow believers to consider the impact of the Trump administration's aggressive deportation policies.
item.dc:creator: PETER SMITH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: University of Minnesota student who was detained by ICE sues for immediate release
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — A University of Minnesota graduate business student who's being held by Immigration and Customs Enforcement is suing for his immediate release, saying his arrest violated his rights and he's been given little explanation for why he's being held.
item.dc:creator: STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Dressing sold at Trader Joe's in Florida recalled over undeclared allergens
item.description: A California company has issued a voluntary recall for a dressing distributed to 18 states and sold under the Trader Joe's private label.
item.dc:creator: Mac Bell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Fourth US soldier missing in Lithuania found dead
item.description: A fourth American soldier who went missing after their vehicle became submerged in a bog during training in the country was found dead Tuesday, the White House announced.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, KRON4

item.title: Ohio school district reverts to birth names, catching students, teachers off guard
item.description: Students at Columbus City Schools with preferred names in the district's system had their names reverted back to match their birth certificates.
item.dc:creator: Delaney Ruth, Fox 5

item.title: Odds of a recession are rising, top economists warn
item.description: Goldman Sachs now sees a 35% chance of a U.S. recession in the next 12 months, up from 20% previously.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Bondi directs DOJ to seek death penalty in Luigi Mangione case
item.description: U.S. Attorney General Pam Bondi has directed the Department of Justice to seek the death penalty against Luigi Mangione, the man accused of murdering UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson last year.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Nationwide restaurant chain files for bankruptcy, closes locations
item.description: A nationwide restaurant chain billed as “The Original Gastrobar” has filed for bankruptcy for a second time and has closed more than a dozen locations.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KTLA 5

item.title: Almost 40 percent of Americans report facing extreme weather: Survey
item.description: Nearly 40 percent of Americans experienced extreme weather last year — and those who did were more likely to be concerned about climate change, according to new polling.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: Jury selection begins in Karen Read retrial over the death of her Boston police boyfriend
item.description: Read is accused of striking her boyfriend, Boston police officer John O’Keefe, with her SUV and leaving him to die in a snowstorm outside of a house party.
item.dc:creator: Michael Casey, Fox 5

item.title: Booker talks through the night in marathon floor speech to protest Trump
item.description: Sen. Cory Booker (D-N.J.) pulled an all-nighter, delivering a marathon speech on the Senate floor that lasted well into Tuesday morning in a push to combat the Trump administration’s policies. Booker, a member of Democratic leadership, rose to speak around 7 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Musk says he sent MAGA influencer Ashley St. Clair $2.5 million, open to paternity test
item.description: Musk’s comments came after St. Clair was seen selling her Tesla, saying she was doing so after Musk allegedly cut childcare payments.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, ABC 4

item.title: NASA's newly returned astronauts say they would fly on Boeing's Starliner capsule again
item.description: CAPE CANAVERAL, Fla. (AP) — NASA’s celebrity astronauts Butch Wilmore and Suni Williams said Monday that they hold themselves partly responsible for what went wrong on their space sprint-turned-marathon and would fly on Boeing's Starliner again.
item.dc:creator: MARCIA DUNN, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Nonprofit groups and Democrats sue Trump administration over election executive order
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President Donald Trump's executive order seeking to overhaul the nation's elections faced its first legal challenges Monday as the Democratic National Committee and a pair of nonprofits filed two separate lawsuits calling it unconstitutional.
item.dc:creator: ALI SWENSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Apparent explosion, fire injures about a dozen in Detroit apartment building
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — About a dozen people have been injured, including six children, following an apparent explosion and fire Monday morning at a 2-story apartment building in Detroit. The explosion was reported about 4 a.m., according to fire officials.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 5 players, 2 coaches ejected after Pistons-Timberwolves skirmish in 2nd quarter
item.description: Five players and two coaches were ejected after a fight broke out in the second quarter in the Minnesota Timberwolves' victory over the Detroit Pistons on Sunday night.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK DONNELLY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 3 killed, 3 hurt from same family when tree falls on car during storm
item.description: Three people were killed after a tree struck their vehicle in Michigan as strong storms moved through the area Sunday, deputies say.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Van Gilder, Fox 5

item.title: Cause of death determined for 3 young American women who died at Belize resort
item.description: A cause of death has been determined for three young women from the United States found dead in their hotel room at a resort in San Pedro, Belize, late last month, NBC News reported. The trio were discovered deceased on Feb.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, KTLA 5
